note: Live mission schedule. Updated based on NASA blog and mission audio.

crewSchedule.label: Launch & ascent
crewSchedule.desc: SLS liftoff, main-engine cutoff ~8 min after launch.

crewSchedule.label: Orbital insertion burns
crewSchedule.desc: ICPS perigee-raise burn (~49 min), then high-Earth orbit insertion (~1 hr).

crewSchedule.label: Post-insertion checks
crewSchedule.desc: Remove launch suits, reconfigure cabin for living and work.

crewSchedule.label: Proximity operations demo
crewSchedule.desc: ICPS becomes docking target; crew practices manual flying toward and around it.

crewSchedule.label: Orion systems checkout
crewSchedule.desc: Test potable water dispenser, toilet, CO₂ removal system.

crewSchedule.label: Crew meal
crewSchedule.desc: First meal in orbit — test food rehydration system.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Brief rest period (~4 hours) after first day in space.

crewSchedule.label: Orbital manoeuvre & DSN checkout
crewSchedule.desc: Engine firing for correct TLI orbital geometry; emergency comms test on Deep Space Network.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: After perigee raise burn, crew returns to sleep for ~4.5 hours before first full day in space.

crewSchedule.label: Wake & meal
crewSchedule.desc: Morning routine and breakfast.

crewSchedule.label: Flywheel exercise setup & workout
crewSchedule.desc: Wiseman and Glover set up flywheel exercise device and perform first mission workouts.

crewSchedule.label: Koch & Hansen exercise
crewSchedule.desc: Koch and Hansen exercise on the flywheel device.

crewSchedule.label: TLI burn preparation
crewSchedule.desc: Koch prepares Orion's orbital manoeuvring system engine (~6,000 lbs thrust) for translunar injection.

crewSchedule.label: Translunar injection burn
crewSchedule.desc: The TLI burn — sets course for the Moon and establishes the free-return trajectory for Earth return on Day 10.

crewSchedule.label: Post-TLI checks & acclimation
crewSchedule.desc: Verify spacecraft health after the critical burn; confirm trajectory; crew acclimation time.

crewSchedule.label: First video downlink
crewSchedule.desc: First space-to-ground video communication session with Mission Control.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Full rest period after TLI day.

crewSchedule.label: Wake & meal
crewSchedule.desc: Morning routine and breakfast.

crewSchedule.label: Trajectory correction burn #1 prep
crewSchedule.desc: Hansen prepares for first of three outbound course-correction burns.

crewSchedule.label: Outbound trajectory correction burn #1
crewSchedule.desc: First mid-course correction ensures Orion stays on target for the Moon.

crewSchedule.label: CPR & medical kit demo
crewSchedule.desc: Glover, Koch, and Hansen demonstrate CPR procedures in microgravity; Wiseman and Glover check out medical kit.

crewSchedule.label: DSN emergency comms test
crewSchedule.desc: Koch tests Orion's emergency communications system on the Deep Space Network.

crewSchedule.label: Lunar flyby rehearsal
crewSchedule.desc: Entire crew rehearses choreography for Day 6 lunar flyby scientific observations.

crewSchedule.label: Exercise & personal time
crewSchedule.desc: Crew exercise and downtime.

crewSchedule.label: Video downlink
crewSchedule.desc: Scheduled space-to-ground video session.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Full rest period.

crewSchedule.label: Wake & meal
crewSchedule.desc: Morning routine and breakfast.

crewSchedule.label: Trajectory correction burn #2 prep
crewSchedule.desc: Preparation for second outbound course correction.

crewSchedule.label: Outbound trajectory correction burn #2
crewSchedule.desc: Second mid-course correction refines lunar approach path.

crewSchedule.label: Lunar target study
crewSchedule.desc: Each astronaut reviews geographic targets for Day 6 imagery — exact targets based on launch day/time.

crewSchedule.label: Celestial photography
crewSchedule.desc: Crew photographs celestial bodies from Orion's windows.

crewSchedule.label: Exercise
crewSchedule.desc: Flywheel workout session.

crewSchedule.label: Video downlink & personal time
crewSchedule.desc: Video call with ground and free time.

crewSchedule.label: Crew meal
crewSchedule.desc: Evening meal.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Full rest period.

crewSchedule.label: Wake & meal
crewSchedule.desc: Morning routine and breakfast.

crewSchedule.label: Spacesuit pressure testing
crewSchedule.desc: Test donning suits quickly, pressurizing them, installing seats, eating/drinking through helmet port.

crewSchedule.label: Crew meal & rest
crewSchedule.desc: Midday break.

crewSchedule.label: Trajectory correction burn #3 prep
crewSchedule.desc: Preparation for final outbound course correction before lunar flyby.

crewSchedule.label: Outbound trajectory correction burn #3
crewSchedule.desc: Final outbound mid-course correction. Orion enters the lunar sphere of influence today.

crewSchedule.label: Exercise
crewSchedule.desc: Flywheel workout session.

crewSchedule.label: Video downlink & personal time
crewSchedule.desc: Video call with ground and free time.

crewSchedule.label: Crew meal
crewSchedule.desc: Evening meal.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Full rest period before lunar flyby day.

crewSchedule.label: Wake early — lunar flyby day
crewSchedule.desc: Early wake-up for the historic lunar flyby.

crewSchedule.label: Crew meal & flyby prep
crewSchedule.desc: Breakfast and final preparation for close lunar approach.

crewSchedule.label: Lunar flyby — photography & observations
crewSchedule.desc: Closest approach: 4,000–6,000 miles from lunar surface. Moon appears basketball-sized at arm's length. Crew photographs and records observations.

crewSchedule.label: Far-side transit — loss of signal
crewSchedule.desc: Orion passes behind the Moon. Communications blackout lasting 30–50 minutes.

crewSchedule.label: Post-flyby science & observation
crewSchedule.desc: Continued photography and recordings after reacquiring signal. First humans to see parts of the lunar far side with their own eyes.

crewSchedule.label: Exercise & crew meal
crewSchedule.desc: Post-flyby meal and exercise.

crewSchedule.label: Video downlink
crewSchedule.desc: Share lunar flyby experience with Mission Control and the world.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Rest period after the historic flyby.

crewSchedule.label: Wake & meal
crewSchedule.desc: Morning routine. Orion exits the lunar sphere of influence today.

crewSchedule.label: Crew lunar debrief
crewSchedule.desc: Ground scientists speak with crew while the lunar flyby experience is fresh.

crewSchedule.label: Return trajectory correction burn #1
crewSchedule.desc: First of three return course corrections to adjust path home.

crewSchedule.label: Exercise
crewSchedule.desc: Flywheel workout session.

crewSchedule.label: Off-duty time
crewSchedule.desc: Largely off-duty day for rest before final return phase tasks.

crewSchedule.label: Crew meal
crewSchedule.desc: Evening meal.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Extended rest period.

crewSchedule.label: Wake & meal
crewSchedule.desc: Morning routine and breakfast.

crewSchedule.label: Radiation shelter demonstration
crewSchedule.desc: Crew builds a protective shelter using Orion supplies — demonstrating shielding from solar particle events.

crewSchedule.label: Manual piloting demonstration
crewSchedule.desc: Test Orion manual control: target centering, tail-to-Sun attitude, 6-DOF vs 3-DOF manoeuvres.

crewSchedule.label: Exercise
crewSchedule.desc: Flywheel workout session.

crewSchedule.label: Video downlink & crew meal
crewSchedule.desc: Video call with ground and evening meal.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Full rest period.

crewSchedule.label: Wake & meal
crewSchedule.desc: Morning routine and breakfast.

crewSchedule.label: Re-entry & splashdown procedure review
crewSchedule.desc: Crew studies re-entry procedures and talks with flight control team.

crewSchedule.label: Return trajectory correction burn #2
crewSchedule.desc: Course correction to keep Orion on target for splashdown zone.

crewSchedule.label: Waste system & garment testing
crewSchedule.desc: Test backup waste collection systems; fit-check orthostatic intolerance compression garments.

crewSchedule.label: Exercise & personal time
crewSchedule.desc: Final workout session and personal time.

crewSchedule.label: Crew meal
crewSchedule.desc: Evening meal — last full evening in space.

crewSchedule.label: Sleep period
crewSchedule.desc: Final rest period before re-entry day.

crewSchedule.label: Wake early — re-entry day
crewSchedule.desc: Early wake-up for the final day of the mission.

crewSchedule.label: Final stow & cabin prep
crewSchedule.desc: Return cabin to launch configuration: stow equipment, install seats.

crewSchedule.label: Don spacesuits for re-entry
crewSchedule.desc: Crew dons OCSS pressure suits, closes visors, and straps in for atmospheric entry.

crewSchedule.label: Service module separation
crewSchedule.desc: Crew module separates from European Service Module prior to atmospheric entry.

crewSchedule.label: Atmospheric re-entry
crewSchedule.desc: Heat shield faces temperatures up to ~2,760 °C (5,000 °F) during re-entry at ~40,000 km/h.

crewSchedule.label: Parachute descent
crewSchedule.desc: Drogue chutes slow to ~494 km/h, then three main chutes deploy — final descent at ~27 km/h.

crewSchedule.label: Splashdown & recovery
crewSchedule.desc: Splashdown in the Pacific Ocean. NASA and U.S. Navy recovery teams retrieve crew and capsule.
